items.title: Αδωνις διαψεύδει Μητσοτάκη;
items.snippet: Διαψεύδει τον πρωθυπουργό ο υπουργός Υγείας; «Δεν είναι ότι δεν θέλουμε, δεν βρίσκουμε [νοσηλευτές]», δήλωνε ο Κυριάκος Μητσοτάκης στην αιφνιδιαστική πρωτοχρονιάτικη επίσκεψή του στο νοσοκομείο «Γ. Γεννηματάς». Σε χθεσινή ανάρτησή του ο Αδωνις Γεωργιάδης ανακοίνωνε 15.995 οριστικ

items.title: Επίθεση ΣΥΡΙΖΑ σε Τσίπρα για τις αποχωρήσεις βουλευτών: «Προμελετημένο σχέδιο διάλυσης του κόμματος»

items.title: Περιοδεία Μητσοτάκη με άρωμα… εκλογών: Εμφανής πλέον ο φόβος για το κόμμα Σαμαρά
items.snippet: Την παρατεταμένη εκλογική περίοδο επιβεβαίωσε ο πρωθυπουργός, ξορκίζοντας πάντως τις πρόωρες κάλπες. Η αυτοδυναμία παραμένει (θεωρητικά) ο στόχος, εμφανής πλέον στο Μαξίμου ο φόβος για το υπό ίδρυση κόμμα Σαμαρά, καρφί από Μητσοτάκη με αναδρομή στο 1993

items.title: ΗΠΑ: Το νόμισμα του ενός δολαρίου αποκτά το πρόσωπο του… Ντόναλντ Τραμπ
items.snippet: Πέρα από το πρόσωπο του Τραμπ, στο νέο μονοδόλαρο θα αναγράφεται η λέξη «Ελευθερία», η φράση «In God we trust» («Εμπιστευόμαστε τον Θεό»), κ.ά.

items.title: Μουντιάλ 2026: Οι παίκτες της Αργεντινής σήκωσαν πανό για τα νησιά Φόκλαντ – Κίνδυνος για βαριά καμπάνα από τη FIFA
items.snippet: Μετά τη λήξη του ημιτελικού με την Αγγλία, οι παίκτες της Αλμπισελέστε πανηγύρισαν σηκώνοντας πανό που έγραφε «Τα νησιά Φόκλαντ ανήκουν στην Αργεντινή».

items.title: Γλυφάδα: Ξεκίνησαν οι ψεκασμοί μετά από κρούσμα του ιού του Δυτικού Νείλου [βίντεο]

lead.title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. and Iran Exchange Strikes for 5th Straight Day
lead.snippet: Though both sides appeared to be digging in, a top Iranian official seemed to leave the door open for a deal, saying, “We must fear neither war nor negotiations.”

items.title: Ukraine Pounds Russian Ships in Its Campaign to Cut Off Crimea
items.snippet: After striking roads and railways, Kyiv is now focusing on sea routes as it tries to disrupt fuel supplies and pressure Russia to end the war.

items.title: He Has a $25 Million Bounty on His Head but Is Also a U.S. Partner in Venezuela
items.snippet: A list of alleged crimes has not prevented Diosdado Cabello, and other Venezuelan officials, from working with a U.S. government that once hounded them.

items.title: For Argentina, a World Cup Semifinal Against England Isn’t Just Soccer
items.snippet: Wednesday’s contest for a spot in the final carries the weight of a 1986 contentious clash, a war over the Falklands and a national mythology built around Diego Maradona.

items.title: For Israel, the U.S.-Iran Hostilities Have Created an Uneasy Limbo
items.snippet: Officials see a return to full-blown war as preferable to an agreement that fails to curb the threats Iran poses to Israel. Meanwhile, they wait.

items.title: France Votes to Allow Assisted Dying
items.snippet: Lawmakers approved a bill that will allow doctors to help terminally ill patients to die, adding France to a short list of nations that allow the practice.

items.title: Wildfires Rage in Ontario as Smoke Affects Toronto’s Air Quality
items.snippet: Air quality in Canada’s largest city was at times the worst in the world on Wednesday, as wildfires across Ontario sent smoke drifting into New York City and beyond.

items.title: Australia to Impose Energy and Water Guardrails on Data Centers Amid A.I. Boom
items.snippet: The country will also seek to protect the rights of creators of work used to train artificial intelligence models, as it aims to impose parameters on the growing industry.

items.title: As Argentina Tries to Win the World Cup, the Rest of Latin America Cheers Against It
items.snippet: Rather than rally behind the tournament’s last Latin American team, other countries in the region are confronting Argentina’s fraught claims to its own identity.

items.title: U.S. Presses China to Free American Seismologist Accused of Spying
items.snippet: President Trump has urged Xi Jinping to release the scientist, Youlin Chen, the man’s family says. Dr. Chen studied the detection of North Korean nuclear tests.

items.title: Missing From Prince Harry’s UK Trip: A Reunion With Prince William
items.snippet: Harry is clearly repairing his relationship with King Charles III, but the rift with his brother remains unresolved.

items.title: Six Dead in an Elevator After Fire in Central Brussels
items.snippet: After a fire broke out at the Oxy building in central Brussels on Tuesday morning, six bodies were found in an elevator.

items.title: Ann Widdecombe Was Killed in ‘Targeted Attack,’ UK Police Say
items.snippet: Counterterrorism police said that they were continuing to interview a 28-year-old man accused of killing Ann Widdecombe, a prominent member of the right-wing Reform U.K. party.

items.title: Deportations by India Cause Tension Along Border with Bangladesh
items.snippet: India is driving out Bangladeshi immigrants it says are in the country illegally. Bangladesh is pushing back, leaving people stranded along the border amid increasing tensions between the countries.

items.title: Hong Kong Police Raid Independent Bookstores and Arrest 5 People
items.snippet: Other booksellers have been detained in recent months as part of a broad national security crackdown.

lead.title: Lululemon backs nylon-recycling startup Syntetica in $30M Series A
lead.snippet: Syntetica, a French startup that has developed a novel approach to recycling nylon, has already obtained big-name partners and investors.

items.title: Applied Computing wants to give oil and gas operators an AI model for the entire plant
items.snippet: Applied Computing has raised a $20M Series A to build a foundation AI model for the oil, gas and petrochemical industry.

items.title: Why Greylock capped its new fund at $1.5B when it says it could have raised more
items.snippet: By keeping the number of investments to about 25 per fund, Greylock aims to remain what it calls "the most important partner" to its founders.

items.title: Microsoft is reportedly training salespeople to talk down OpenAI and Anthropic
items.snippet: Microsoft is looking to sell its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective than its competitors' models.

items.title: Tesla driver in fatal Texas crash pressed accelerator 100%, NTSB confirms
items.snippet: The safety board confirmed Tesla's account of the crash, which the company shared days after it happened last month.

items.title: Daniel Ek’s body-scanning startup Neko Health raises another $700M
items.snippet: Neko Health has developed proprietary body-scanning technology, which it couples with bloodwork, to assess a person's health.

items.title: Amid hardware legal battle, OpenAI releases a $230 keyboard for Codex
items.snippet: OpenAI, which is in the middle of a legal battle with Apple over hardware trade theft allegations, just released a light-up keyboard designed to be paired with its agentic coding app.

items.title: Microsoft patches bug in video game Age of Empires II
items.snippet: The vulnerability in the decades-old game could have allowed hackers to take over victims’ computers with a malicious game invite.

items.title: Apple bans home services from its upcoming Maps ads
items.snippet: Apple has published the policies governing its upcoming Maps advertising business, revealing a strategy that differs from Google’s. The new rules prohibit home services businesses like plumbers, electricians, locksmiths, and roofers from advertising on Apple Maps, along with seve

items.title: SpaceX falls to $135 IPO price ahead of Starship launch
items.snippet: The stock has steadily fallen from the euphoric post-IPO high, showing that markets may be sobering up to the promises CEO Elon Musk made before and after SpaceX went public.

items.title: Thinking Machines amps up its bet against one-size-fits-all AI with its first open model, Inkling
items.snippet: It's the company's first public proof point after a year and a half spent building AI infrastructure largely out of public view.

items.title: Phone maker OnePlus reportedly plans to wind down US and Europe operations
items.snippet: OnePlus could also wind down its operations in India by 2027.

items.title: Google’s biggest clean power project is 40 miles north of xAI’s unpermitted gas power plant
items.snippet: Google's biggest solar and battery project stands in sharp contrast with xAI's nearby unpermitted power plant.

items.title: Hack suggests AI music generator Suno scraped YouTube for training data
items.snippet: The hacker used an employee's credentials to access source code, which revealed how Suno scraped decades of audio.

items.title: Whatnot acquires Shaped to power real-time live shopping recommendations
items.snippet: Livestream shopping platform Whatnot has acquired AI startup Shaped, a machine learning company focused on real-time recommendations and search. The deal will bolster Whatnot’s personalization and discovery features as it expands into new product categories.

lead.title: Αγγλία - Αργεντινή: Η σφαλιάρα του Μπέλινγκχαμ στον Μπάρκο που προκάλεσε σύρραξη (vid)

items.snippet: H UEFA έκανε γνωστές τις ώρες των αγώνων του Παναθηναϊκού με την Πάκσι.

items.snippet: Ο Λιονέλ Μέσι κατέγραψε ένα ακόμη απίθανο ρεκόρ στο ματς της Αργεντινής με την Αγγλία για τα ημιτελικά του Μουντιάλ.

items.title: Ο Άρης έκανε το… mega deal με Ερλ Ρόμπινσον!
items.snippet: Ο Άρης ολοκληρώνει μια σπουδαία μεταγραφή καθώς ο Τζερεμάια Ερλ Ρόμπινσον (25, 2.06) θα πρέπει να θεωρείται οριστικά δικός του με την ενεργοποίηση του διετούς συμβολαίου!

items.title: Άι-Χανούμ: Η ξεχασμένη ελληνική πόλη στην καρδιά του Αφγανιστάν (vid)
items.snippet: Πάνω από 4.000 χλμ. μακριά από το Αιγαίο, τα ερείπια της αρχαίας πόλης Άι-Χανούμ στο Αφγανιστάν μαρτυρούν το πιο απομακρυσμένο σύνορο του ελληνιστικού πολιτισμού στην Ασία.

lead.title: ‘The Odyssey’ Review: Passion in Every Frame
lead.snippet: Few directors close the divide between the art film and the blockbuster like Christopher Nolan.

items.title: Archaeologists Unearth a 2,400-Year-Old Sanctuary for the Worship of Odysseus
items.snippet: The ruins of a 2,400-year-old complex suggest that, to the Greeks of antiquity, Homer’s protagonist was not just a character in a poem but a figure of genuine devotion.

items.title: Octavia Spencer Believed in Herself Even When Hollywood Didn’t
items.snippet: It took years for her to be cast as “the main character,” but she considers that the industry’s loss. Her latest series is the action comedy “Ride or Die.”

items.title: Meet Earth’s Newest Monkey. (And Check Out Its Orange Lips.)
items.snippet: It is only the fifth new African monkey identified as a new species in the past 75 years.

items.title: Michelangelo’s Forgotten Renaissance Masterpiece
items.snippet: Tired of the David? A trip across Florence to see the small Santo Spirito Crucifix, a tender vision in old wood, is one of the best decisions you could make in your art-journeying life.

items.title: New Jersey Couple Aids Meteorite Discovery After it Crashes Through Their Roof
items.snippet: A New Jersey couple helped scientists uncover the makeup of an unusual asteroid after it fell into their bedroom.

items.title: Why Did a Major Museum’s Bong Show Go Up in Smoke?
items.snippet: After the Toledo Museum of Art canceled a pioneering exhibition of glass cannabis pipes, many people wondered what lay behind the decision.

items.title: Canada Wildfire Air Pollution and Heat Wave Combo Pose Heightened Health Risks
items.snippet: Days that are both extremely hot and polluted come with higher risks of respiratory ailments and other health hazards.

items.title: ‘Heartstopper Forever’: Growing Up, and Staying True to Themselves
items.snippet: Like “Heated Rivalry,” this beloved Netflix romance has a big-hearted ethos. It’s bringing that spirit to a new film that rounds out a teenage TV hit.

items.title: New Winged Robot Can Fly and Swim Like a Puffin
items.snippet: Inspired by the physical feats of diving birds, researchers have created a robot that can plunge into the water and flap back into the air.

items.title: On ‘Caribenya,’ Lido Pimienta Found a Lifeline
items.snippet: On Lido Pimienta’s new album, “Caribenya,” the Colombian Canadian musician sought a warmer, more complex depiction of Caribbean culture. Creating it helped her rediscover the beauty of living.

items.title: Researchers Claim to Have Solved the Perplexing ‘Reverse Sprinkler’ Problem That Stumped Feynman
items.snippet: Physicists have debated which way a submerged sprinkler sucking in water would spin. Careful experiments provide an answer.

items.title: Robert Wilson’s Watermill Center Without Robert Wilson
items.snippet: “When I’m no longer living, can this place exist?” Robert Wilson wondered in a film about his Long Island art incubator. This month, its annual festival will go on without him.

items.title: A Sweet Surprise: Scientists Find Sugar Deep in Our Galaxy
items.snippet: It’s the first time a sugar molecule has been detected in interstellar space. The discovery provides tantalizing new clues into how life may have arisen on Earth.

items.title: ‘The Potluck’ Review: The Greensboro Massacre, and a Heavy Legacy to Bear
items.snippet: César Alvarez’s ambitious new work is a mixed musical meal: It wants to be a call to revolutionary action but is surprisingly lacking in it.
